title: Circular Parent-Child Hierarchy Detected in Architecture
description: Architecture diagram generation detected circular parent-child relationships (e.g., VM → NIC → VM). This indicates a schema issue or incorrect hierarchy definition that results in confusing visual representation.

implementation.description: Detect when parent_resource_id of a child resource points to the same resource as the child's parent_resource_id of the parent
implementation.sql_query: SELECT DISTINCT child.resource_name, child.resource_type, parent.resource_name, parent.resource_type FROM resources child JOIN resources parent ON child.parent_resource_id = parent.id WHERE child.experiment_id = ? AND parent.experiment_id = ? AND parent.parent_resource_id = child.id
implementation.logic: If query returns results, circular hierarchy exists

remediation: Review resource hierarchy definitions in the Terraform code
Ensure parent-child relationships are acyclic (no resource can be its own ancestor)
Update generate_hierarchical_diagram.py to detect and flatten circular hierarchies before rendering
Consider whether the hierarchy represents actual AWS/Azure/GCP structure or is a modeling artifact

automation.validation_rule: Check for cycles using depth-first search
